covered_report_window: Tuesday, June 23, 2026 after U.S. market close and Wednesday, June 24, 2026 before U.S. market open.
market_context_summary: U.S. equities were under pressure during the day, led by a tech and semiconductor selloff, while FedEx was the most watched report in a light immediate earnings window.

ranked_companies.expected_driver: Backlog conversion and FY27 margin tone
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: All three passes Lean Up; tight spread
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Backlog growth, order momentum, cash-heavy balance sheet and Q4 conversion commentary create the cleanest upside setup, though liquidity and tariff/mix risks keep confidence at Medium.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Digrin lists EPS near $0.20 on revenue near $209M; Benzinga shows EPS near $0.16 and revenue near $205M.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Backlog converts cleanly, orders stay positive, margins hold despite component/tariff pressure, and FY27 strategic targets sound credible.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Gross margin mix slips, tariff mitigation disappoints, orders slow after stadium/project conversion, or small-cap profit taking overwhelms the print.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Options evidence limited; small-cap liquidity raises event-volatility risk more than listed options signal.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Sparse analyst coverage; setup relies more on company backlog/orders than broad estimate revisions.

ranked_companies.pass_agreement: All three passes Lean Up; modest spread
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: The setup leans positive because Paychex enters with durable revenue growth, Paycor-driven Management Solutions expansion and margin support after a large drawdown.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Consensus is roughly $1.31-$1.33 EPS on about $1.60-$1.63B revenue, with the call at 9:30 a.m. ET.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Management Solutions growth, adjusted margin, cash return and FY27 integration language clear a lowered stock-price bar.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Revenue quality excluding Paycor, PEO/insurance enrollment, interest expense or integration amortization disappoints.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: No strong options signal found; stock is far below its prior-year high, reducing but not removing downside risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Published previews show Q4 revenue expected to rise about 12% year over year; current estimates cluster near $1.31-$1.33 EPS.

ranked_companies.pass_agreement: Two Lean Up, one Neutral / No Edge
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Worthington has a constructive but not forceful upside setup because Q3 showed resilient adjusted EPS/EBITDA growth and acquisition support, while Q4 expectations are no longer low.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: MarketWatch/Benzinga-type estimate sources cluster around roughly $1.06 EPS and $386M revenue for the current quarter.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Building Products volume, LSI contribution, WAVE/equity income and buybacks support another clean quarter.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Consumer Products volumes, construction-cycle softness, acquisition costs or WAVE normalization limit enthusiasm.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Limited options evidence; moderate liquidity and industrial cyclicality keep reversal risk near the allocation cutoff.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Consensus target/estimate sources imply modest upside but not a deeply discounted setup after recent gains.

ranked_companies.pass_agreement: All three passes Lean Down; reversal risk moderate-high
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: KB Home leans down because the market is likely to focus on Q2 gross margin, orders and guide durability after Q1 revenue, deliveries and margins fell sharply.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Company guided Q2 housing revenue to $1.05-$1.15B, deliveries to 2,250-2,450 and gross margin to 15.0%-15.6%.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Built-to-order mix, lower cancellations, faster build times, buybacks and book value support produce relief.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Affordability pressure, weak orders, price cuts, margin erosion or lower FY26 housing revenue guide dominate.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Specific implied move not reliable; homebuilder cyclicality and low expectations raise squeeze/relief risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Public estimate pages show a cautious/neutral analyst backdrop; company guidance is the key benchmark.

ranked_companies.pass_agreement: Two Lean Down, one Neutral / No Edge
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: FedEx has downside skew because a strong year-to-date rally and high expectations leave little room for a merely solid Q4 or cautious post-spin fiscal 2027 guide.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Previews cluster around $5.90-$6.02 adjusted EPS and roughly $24B revenue, with options implying about a 7% move.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Network 2.0/DRIVE savings, Express yield, free cash flow and post-spin guidance exceed elevated expectations.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Guidance fails to justify the rally, Freight separation noise, trade/tariff demand pressure or margin costs disappoint.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Investopedia and Optionslam/MarketChameleon-type data point to about a 7%-7.5% event move; analyst sentiment is bullish, raising opposite-direction risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Zacks/Yahoo previews cite modest upward FY26 EPS revisions; analyst ratings are mostly constructive, creating a high bar.

qualified_names.why: Highest consensus direction score, low disparity, reversal risk below 40, and evidence centered on backlog/orders.
High-quality evidence, score above 30, reversal risk below 40, and lowered stock-price bar after drawdown.

excluded_high_reversal_or_weak_names.reason: Absolute score above 30, but reversal risk is 46 because expectations are already weak and buybacks/BTO relief could squeeze higher.
Lean Down score, but reversal risk is 58 due to bullish analyst sentiment, cost-savings upside and elevated options move.
Reversal risk is acceptable but absolute direction score is below the >30 allocation threshold.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: All three passes leaned up around backlog/order conversion; spread was tight.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: Backlog growth, order momentum, cash-heavy balance sheet and Q4 conversion commentary create the cleanest upside setup, though liquidity and tariff/mix risks keep confidence at Medium.
detailed_company_sections.market_expectation_summary: Digrin lists EPS near $0.20 on revenue near $209M; Benzinga shows EPS near $0.16 and revenue near $205M.
detailed_company_sections.upside_case_summary: Backlog converts cleanly, orders stay positive, margins hold despite component/tariff pressure, and FY27 strategic targets sound credible.
detailed_company_sections.downside_case_summary: Gross margin mix slips, tariff mitigation disappoints, orders slow after stadium/project conversion, or small-cap profit taking overwhelms the print.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Low-moderate; tariffs, project mix and small-cap liquidity can reverse a good headline.
detailed_company_sections.options_or_positioning_read: Options evidence limited; small-cap liquidity raises event-volatility risk more than listed options signal.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Sparse analyst coverage; setup relies more on company backlog/orders than broad estimate revisions.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Clean backlog conversion and stable margin commentary produce a positive but not explosive reaction.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Orders, backlog and margin all beat, with FY27 commentary supporting growth targets.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Tariff/mix pressure or weaker orders dominate the strong backlog story.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_view_rationale: Included: score above 30, reversal risk below 40, low disparity and sufficient evidence quality.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: All three passes leaned up, with the strongest agreement around Paycor integration, margin support and a lowered price bar.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: The setup leans positive because Paychex enters with durable revenue growth, Paycor-driven Management Solutions expansion and margin support after a large drawdown.
detailed_company_sections.market_expectation_summary: Consensus is roughly $1.31-$1.33 EPS on about $1.60-$1.63B revenue, with the call at 9:30 a.m. ET.
detailed_company_sections.upside_case_summary: Management Solutions growth, adjusted margin, cash return and FY27 integration language clear a lowered stock-price bar.
detailed_company_sections.downside_case_summary: Revenue quality excluding Paycor, PEO/insurance enrollment, interest expense or integration amortization disappoints.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Low-moderate; integration and organic-growth quality are the main ways a headline beat could fade.
detailed_company_sections.options_or_positioning_read: No strong options signal found; stock is far below its prior-year high, reducing but not removing downside risk.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Published previews show Q4 revenue expected to rise about 12% year over year; current estimates cluster near $1.31-$1.33 EPS.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Q4 roughly clears consensus and FY27 integration language is constructive, supporting a modest positive reaction.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Organic-plus-Paycor growth and adjusted margin both surprise positively.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: The market looks through acquired growth and focuses on integration costs, PEO softness or debt interest.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_view_rationale: Included: score above 30, reversal risk below 40, high evidence quality and low disparity.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: Two passes leaned up and one was neutral because the Q3 momentum is real but expectations are less forgiving.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: Worthington has a constructive but not forceful upside setup because Q3 showed resilient adjusted EPS/EBITDA growth and acquisition support, while Q4 expectations are no longer low.
detailed_company_sections.market_expectation_summary: MarketWatch/Benzinga-type estimate sources cluster around roughly $1.06 EPS and $386M revenue for the current quarter.
detailed_company_sections.upside_case_summary: Building Products volume, LSI contribution, WAVE/equity income and buybacks support another clean quarter.
detailed_company_sections.downside_case_summary: Consumer Products volumes, construction-cycle softness, acquisition costs or WAVE normalization limit enthusiasm.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Low-moderate; cyclical demand and a higher estimate bar could reverse a clean EPS result.
detailed_company_sections.options_or_positioning_read: Limited options evidence; moderate liquidity and industrial cyclicality keep reversal risk near the allocation cutoff.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Consensus target/estimate sources imply modest upside but not a deeply discounted setup after recent gains.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Another solid quarter supports a mild positive reaction.

detailed_company_sections.bear_case: The market focuses on demand normalization, acquisition costs or Consumer Products softness.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: All three passes leaned down, but all flagged meaningful relief risk from low expectations and capital return.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: KB Home leans down because the market is likely to focus on Q2 gross margin, orders and guide durability after Q1 revenue, deliveries and margins fell sharply.
detailed_company_sections.market_expectation_summary: Company guided Q2 housing revenue to $1.05-$1.15B, deliveries to 2,250-2,450 and gross margin to 15.0%-15.6%.
detailed_company_sections.upside_case_summary: Built-to-order mix, lower cancellations, faster build times, buybacks and book value support produce relief.
detailed_company_sections.downside_case_summary: Affordability pressure, weak orders, price cuts, margin erosion or lower FY26 housing revenue guide dominate.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Moderate-high; expectations are low enough that BTO progress or buyback support could squeeze the stock higher.
detailed_company_sections.options_or_positioning_read: Specific implied move not reliable; homebuilder cyclicality and low expectations raise squeeze/relief risk.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Public estimate pages show a cautious/neutral analyst backdrop; company guidance is the key benchmark.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Orders and margin remain soft enough to pressure the stock.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: BTO orders, cancellations and build times improve enough to create relief despite weak margins.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Q2 misses the margin/order bar and management trims or softens full-year commentary.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_view_rationale: Excluded: reversal risk is above 40 despite a score beyond 30.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: Two passes leaned down because of the high bar; one was neutral because transformation savings could still clear expectations.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: FedEx has downside skew because a strong year-to-date rally and high expectations leave little room for a merely solid Q4 or cautious post-spin fiscal 2027 guide.
detailed_company_sections.market_expectation_summary: Previews cluster around $5.90-$6.02 adjusted EPS and roughly $24B revenue, with options implying about a 7% move.
detailed_company_sections.upside_case_summary: Network 2.0/DRIVE savings, Express yield, free cash flow and post-spin guidance exceed elevated expectations.
detailed_company_sections.downside_case_summary: Guidance fails to justify the rally, Freight separation noise, trade/tariff demand pressure or margin costs disappoint.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: High; bullish analysts, cost-savings optionality and a 7% implied move make a sharp upside reversal plausible.
detailed_company_sections.options_or_positioning_read: Investopedia and Optionslam/MarketChameleon-type data point to about a 7%-7.5% event move; analyst sentiment is bullish, raising opposite-direction risk.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Zacks/Yahoo previews cite modest upward FY26 EPS revisions; analyst ratings are mostly constructive, creating a high bar.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Q4 is decent but post-Freight FY27 commentary is not strong enough for the rally.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Cost savings, Express yields and free cash flow produce a clear beat-and-raise style reaction.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: A cautious post-spin guide or tariff/trade pressure makes the stock retrace part of its rally.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_view_rationale: Excluded: reversal risk is far above 40 and disparity is the highest in the set.

disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
